title: Palantir Artificial Intelligence Platform
summary: Palantir's main AIP product page. AIP connects AI with enterprise data and operations, providing governed access to LLMs and multimodal models. It includes builder tools like AIP Logic, Agent Studio, and AIP Evals for developing production-ready AI-powered workflows, agents, and functions on top of the Ontology.
relevantParts: AIP platform overview connecting AI to enterprise operations
Builder tools: AIP Logic, Agent Studio, AIP Evals
Governed LLM access and security controls

title: Overview - AIP - Palantir Docs
summary: Official AIP documentation overview. AIP provides a full set of capabilities for building with generative AI: governed LLM access, LLM-driven functions, agent creation/management, evaluation suites, and automation definitions. AIP is designed for everyone from developers to frontline users.
relevantParts: Comprehensive AIP capability listing
Connection between AIP and Ontology developer toolchain
Security and governance model for AI operations

title: Semantic search - Ontology augmented generation - Palantir Docs
summary: Official documentation on Ontology Augmented Generation (OAG). Explains how OAG extends RAG by grounding LLMs in the Ontology's decision-centric model that combines data, logic, and actions. Covers semantic search implementation for querying Ontology objects from LLM contexts.
relevantParts: OAG as an extension of RAG grounded in the Ontology
Semantic search over Ontology objects using vector embeddings
Integration of data, logic, and actions for LLM grounding

title: Building with Palantir AIP: Data Tools for RAG / OAG - Palantir Blog
summary: Palantir engineering blog post explaining how to build data tools that enable LLMs to query the Ontology for trusted data. Covers how equipping LLMs with Ontology query capabilities is a significant step in reducing hallucinations. Details the data tool paradigm within OAG.
relevantParts: How data tools allow LLMs to query Ontology objects
Practical implementation of OAG data retrieval
Hallucination reduction through trusted data access

title: Building with Palantir AIP: Logic Tools for RAG/OAG - Palantir Blog
summary: Engineering blog post on logic tools for RAG/OAG. Explains how enterprises can combine forecasting models, optimizers, and other logic assets with LLM reasoning capabilities. Logic tools let LLMs invoke deterministic functions alongside non-deterministic LLM reasoning.
relevantParts: Logic tools combining deterministic computation with LLM reasoning
Integration of forecasting models and optimizers as LLM tools
Enterprise-scale AI that leverages existing logic assets

title: Reducing Hallucinations with the Ontology in Palantir AIP - Palantir Blog
summary: Part 1 of the 'Engineering Responsible AI' series. Explains how AIP prevents hallucinations by giving LLMs the right context from the start via the Ontology. Rather than fixing hallucinations after they occur, OAG retrieves structured objects and their connections, keeping reasoning narrow and accurate.
relevantParts: Prevention-first approach to hallucination reduction
Structured object retrieval vs. traditional text-based RAG
Ontology metadata augmentation for LLM prompt guidance

title: How Palantir AIP Improves LLM Explainability - Palantir Blog
summary: Blog post on improving LLM explainability through AIP. Discusses how AIP Logic tools enable delegation of specific tasks to purpose-built, interpretable tools rather than relying solely on LLM processing, creating composable and auditable AI systems. Covers LLM Debugger for tracking orchestration.
relevantParts: LLM explainability through composable, auditable tool chains
AIP Logic Debugger for tracking LLM orchestration and tool handoffs
Transparent AI reasoning via Ontology grounding

title: Connecting AI to Decisions with the Palantir Ontology - Palantir Blog
summary: Foundational blog post on how the Ontology connects AI to enterprise decision-making. The Ontology brings together data, logic, and actions into a decision-centric model that LLMs can interface with through an extensible tools paradigm. Aggregate user decisions can be leveraged as fine-tuning training data.
relevantParts: Ontology as the bridge between AI and enterprise decisions
Extensible tools paradigm for LLM-Ontology interaction
Decision data as training data for model fine-tuning

title: AIP Logic - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Official documentation for AIP Logic, the no-code development environment for building LLM-powered functions. Tools are the mechanism by which AIP Logic enables LLMs to read/write to the Ontology via three categories: data tools, logic tools, and action tools for powering real-world operations.
relevantParts: No-code environment for building LLM-powered functions
Three tool categories: data, logic, and action
LLM orchestration with Ontology read/write capabilities

title: AIP Agent Studio - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Official documentation for AIP Agent Studio. Allows building interactive assistants (AIP Agents) equipped with enterprise-specific information and tools powered by LLMs, the Ontology, documents, and custom tools. Agents are deployable internally and externally through the Ontology SDK and platform APIs.
relevantParts: Agent Studio for building enterprise AI assistants
Agents leverage Ontology, documents, and custom tools
Internal and external deployment via OSDK and APIs

title: AIP Agent Studio - Tools - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on the tools available in AIP Agent Studio. Includes action tools (ontology edits), object query tools (LLM access to object types), request clarification, commands (interact with Palantir apps on behalf of users), and custom functions. Supports native and prompted tool calling modes.
relevantParts: Tool types: action, object query, clarification, commands, functions
Native vs prompted tool calling modes for agents
Parallel tool calling support in native mode

title: AIP Agent Studio - Core concepts - Palantir Docs
summary: Core concepts documentation for AIP Agent Studio covering how agents use Ontology, document, or custom function-backed context for targeted interactions. Explains RAG within the agent context, where retrieval-augmented generation dynamically provides LLMs with relevant information from external sources.
relevantParts: Agent context types: Ontology, document, custom function
RAG implementation within Agent Studio
Dynamic context provision for enterprise-specific interactions

title: Supported LLMs - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation listing all LLMs supported in Palantir AIP. Includes models from OpenAI (GPT-5.2, GPT-4.1, GPT-4o), Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude 3.5 Sonnet), Google (Gemini), Meta (Llama), xAI, and others. Covers capacity management via TPM and RPM.
relevantParts: Comprehensive list of supported LLM providers and models
Multi-provider support: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, xAI
LLM capacity management with TPM/RPM metrics

title: AIP features - Palantir Docs
summary: Comprehensive listing of all AIP features including AIP Logic, Agent Studio, AIP Evals, AIP Assist, Pipeline Builder LLM node, and more. Provides the complete feature map of how AIP integrates across the Foundry platform for building AI-powered workflows.
relevantParts: Complete AIP feature inventory
Cross-platform integration points
Feature categorization by developer vs. frontline user

title: Platform overview - AIP capabilities - Palantir Docs
summary: Platform overview page focused on AIP capabilities within the broader Foundry platform. Shows how AIP fits into the overall architecture alongside data integration, Ontology, and application layers. Describes AIP as the AI layer that sits on top of the Ontology.
relevantParts: AIP positioning within Foundry platform architecture
Relationship between AIP and Ontology layers
End-to-end capability from data ingestion to AI-powered operations

title: AIP Evals - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for AIP Evals, the testing environment for evaluating LLM-powered function performance. Designed to handle non-deterministic LLM behavior with grid search parameter testing, custom evaluators (no-code, Python, TypeScript, or LLM-as-judge), and execution trace debugging.
relevantParts: LLM evaluation framework for non-deterministic outputs
Grid search for parameter combination testing
Custom evaluators including LLM-as-judge pattern

title: A Palantir Manual for Evaluating Generative AI - Palantir Blog
summary: Palantir's field manual for evaluating generative AI systems. Provides practical guidance on how to systematically assess and improve LLM-powered workflows. Covers evaluation methodology, metrics selection, and operational best practices for responsible AI deployment.
relevantParts: Systematic evaluation methodology for generative AI
Practical guidance on metrics and assessment
Responsible AI deployment best practices

title: Palantir AIP | Testing & Evaluating AI Systems - Palantir Blog
summary: Part 3 of the 'Engineering Responsible AI' series. Covers the journey from AI prototype to production, focusing on systematic testing and evaluation. Discusses how AIP provides tools for generating audit trails, explanations, and model decision evaluations.
relevantParts: Prototype-to-production pathway for responsible AI
Audit trails and model decision explanations
Systematic testing frameworks within AIP

title: Semantic search - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on semantic search in the Ontology. Explains how AI models transform text into vector embeddings for similarity-based retrieval. When embedded text is associated with Ontology objects, search-driven operational workflows become more useful and contextually grounded.
relevantParts: Vector embedding creation from text for semantic search
Association of embeddings with Ontology objects
Implementation approaches: AI Agents, custom functions, document processing

title: Semantic search - Use Palantir-provided models - Palantir Docs
summary: Step-by-step documentation for creating semantic search workflows using Palantir-provided embedding models. Covers the end-to-end process from generating embeddings to performing KNN searches over object types, with the goal of enabling user input to vector conversion and similarity retrieval.
relevantParts: End-to-end semantic search workflow setup
Palantir-provided embedding model usage
KNN search implementation over Ontology object types

title: Semantic search - Use custom models - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for creating semantic search workflows using custom embedding models instead of Palantir-provided ones. Covers how to bring your own embedding model and integrate it with the Ontology's semantic search infrastructure for specialized domain-specific search.
relevantParts: Custom embedding model integration for semantic search
BYOM (Bring Your Own Model) for embeddings
Domain-specific search customization

title: Semantic search - Document processing - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on document processing for semantic search in the Ontology. Covers how PDFs and other documents are chunked and embedded separately for more granular retrieval. Enables users and workflows to find important information across unstructured document content.
relevantParts: PDF and document chunking for semantic search
Embedding individual document chunks separately
Unstructured content access through the Ontology

title: Building with Palantir AIP: Semantic Search - Palantir Blog
summary: Engineering blog post on building semantic search with AIP. Covers generating interactive AI copilots that uncover knowledge within enterprise unstructured data, including actionable insights across ticketing systems, emails, and documents without requiring data duplication or complex integration.
relevantParts: Building AI copilots with semantic search over enterprise data
Accessing insights across ticketing, emails, and documents
No data duplication required for semantic search

title: Pipeline Builder - Transforms - Use LLM node - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for the Use LLM node in Pipeline Builder. Provides a no-code method for executing LLMs on data at scale within data transformation pipelines. Includes pre-engineered prompt templates and supports iterating on prompts before full dataset execution.
relevantParts: No-code LLM execution within data pipelines
Pre-engineered prompt templates for common tasks
Scalable LLM processing integrated with data transformations

title: Foundry platform summary for LLMs - Palantir Docs
summary: A platform summary page specifically designed as context for LLM consumption. This page provides a structured overview of the Foundry platform optimized for LLM understanding, serving as a reference document when LLMs need to understand the Palantir platform architecture.

Structured reference for AI systems to understand Foundry
Meta-approach to AI-readable platform documentation

title: Bring your own model - Register an LLM using function interfaces - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for BYOM (Bring Your Own Model) capability. Shows how to register custom LLMs using function interfaces for use across all Palantir developer products including AIP Logic, Pipeline Builder, Agent Studio, and Workshop. Supports on-premise, cloud, or fine-tuned models.
relevantParts: BYOM registration via function interfaces
Cross-platform LLM integration (Logic, Pipeline Builder, Agent Studio, Workshop)

title: Functions - Models - Language models within functions - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on using language models within Foundry Functions. Covers how to integrate LLM capabilities directly into TypeScript/Python functions that can then be used across the Ontology, enabling programmatic access to LLMs within the function execution context.

title: AIP Logic - Blocks - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on AIP Logic blocks, the building blocks used to construct LLM-powered functions in the no-code environment. Each block represents a step in the LLM orchestration chain, including semantic search blocks for finding closest items based on properties and queries.

title: AIP Logic - Getting started - Palantir Docs

title: AIP Logic - Automate AIP Logic - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on automating AIP Logic functions via Automate. Enables creating automations that apply or stage Ontology edits for human review. Automations can be triggered on existing objects or when new objects are created, bridging AI-generated insights to operational action.

Human-in-the-loop review for AI-generated Ontology edits

summary: Documentation for AIP Assist, an LLM-powered support tool for navigating the Palantir platform. Uses NLP and third-party LLMs trained on Palantir documentation to provide real-time assistance. Includes Developer Assist mode specializing in Foundry APIs and developer examples.

title: AIP Assist - Custom content source best practices - Palantir Docs

title: Deploying Full Spectrum AI in Days: How AIP Bootcamps Work - Palantir Blog
summary: Blog post explaining how AIP Bootcamps work as immersive 1-5 day workshops. Participants go from zero to use case, getting hands-on with LLMs and Generative AI to build MVP deployments. Partners solve problems they have struggled with for years in days through hands-on keyboard sessions.
relevantParts: Rapid AI deployment methodology via bootcamps
Hands-on LLM and generative AI experience
Zero to production use case in 1-5 days

title: Speedrun: Your First Agentic AIP Workflow - Palantir Learn
summary: Tutorial on building a first agentic AIP workflow. Covers the steps to create an end-to-end agentic workflow where AI agents can handle complex workflows autonomously, publishing agents as functions and integrating them into AIP Automate for automated execution.

title: Automate - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for the Automate application that enables business automation through condition-based triggers and effects. Supports scheduled, real-time event-driven, and API-driven automations. Integrates with AIP Logic to automate the execution of LLM-powered functions.

Schedule, event-driven, and API-driven automation modes

title: AIP architecture overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Technical architecture documentation for AIP showing how LLM orchestration, tool calling, and model management are structured. Describes how durable orchestrations can be configured through low-code (AIP Logic) or pro-code (Code Workspaces) interfaces for pipeline-style AI workflows.

Durable orchestration via low-code and pro-code interfaces

title: AI ethics and governance - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on AI ethics and governance within AIP. Covers Palantir's eight core themes of responsible AI development with concrete capabilities and workflows. Includes tools for identifying bias, evaluating model fairness, and monitoring ethical AI concerns during model use.
relevantParts: Eight core themes of responsible AI development
Bias identification and fairness evaluation tools
Governance framework for AI operations

title: AIP security and privacy - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on AIP security and privacy features. Covers access controls, encryption, auditing capabilities for AI operations. Defines what LLMs can and cannot see and do with safe AI handoff functions, particularly important for classified and sensitive data environments.
relevantParts: AI-specific security controls and access management
LLM visibility and action boundary definitions
Safe AI handoff functions for sensitive operations

title: Palantir MCP - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for Palantir's Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration. Enables external AI systems to read data and metadata from Foundry using configured user token permissions. Includes Palantir MCP for ontology builders and Ontology MCP for consumers to safely read/write data.

Two MCP modes: Palantir MCP (builders) and Ontology MCP (consumers)

title: Ontology-Oriented Software Development - Palantir Blog
summary: Blog post on the paradigm of ontology-oriented software development. Explains how the OSDK allows developers to write AIP-powered applications in Python, Java, or TypeScript with built-in access to AIP Logic functions. Covers how AI/LLM functions integrate into existing applications via the OSDK.
relevantParts: Ontology-oriented development paradigm for AI applications
OSDK integration with AIP Logic functions

title: Building with Palantir AIP: The Ontology Software Development Kit - Palantir Blog
summary: Blog post on the Ontology SDK (OSDK). Explains how OSDK allows pulling in objects, actions, and AI-backed functions into new and existing applications without building from scratch. LLM integration, writeback, and generative AI are accessible through the same typed SDK interface.
relevantParts: OSDK for integrating AI-backed functions into applications
Typed interface for LLM, writeback, and generative AI
Accelerated development with minimal code for Ontology access

title: Ontology SDK - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Official documentation for the Ontology SDK. Supports NPM (TypeScript), Pip/Conda (Python), Maven (Java), and OpenAPI spec. Types and functions are generated from the Ontology, enabling developers to query and explore their Ontology directly in their editor with full type safety.
relevantParts: Multi-language SDK: TypeScript, Python, Java, OpenAPI
Type-safe generated code from Ontology definitions
Editor-integrated Ontology exploration

title: AI Infrastructure & Ontology | Palantir & NVIDIA - Palantir Blog
summary: Blog post on the Palantir-NVIDIA partnership for AI infrastructure. NVIDIA accelerated computing, CUDA-X libraries, and Nemotron models are integrated into Palantir platforms via the Ontology. Future integration with NVIDIA Blackwell architecture for accelerated AI workflows.
relevantParts: NVIDIA model integration via the Ontology
Accelerated computing for AI workflows
Blackwell architecture integration roadmap

title: Palantir AIP for Defense
summary: Palantir's AIP for Defense page. AIP unleashes LLMs and AI on defense networks from classified systems to the tactical edge. Provides real-time environment representation from sensitive intelligence data, with security features defining what LLMs can see and do including safe AI handoff functions.
relevantParts: Defense-specific AIP deployment on classified networks
LLM security boundaries for sensitive military data

summary: Documentation for the AIP Agent widget in Workshop. Allows embedding interactive AI assistants directly into operational workflow applications. title: The Context Advantage: How Palantir AIP Operates the Modern Enterprise - Towards AI
summary: Third-party analysis of how AIP uses context advantage to operate enterprises. relevantParts: Context advantage concept for enterprise AI

title: Understanding Palantir's Ontology: Semantic, Kinetic, and Dynamic Layers Explained - Medium
summary: Detailed explanation of Palantir Ontology's three layers. The semantic layer provides business-aware data representation, the kinetic layer enables actions and workflows, and the dynamic layer supports AI-driven decision-making. Together they form the foundation for AI-Ontology integration.
